mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and eases financial conditions, so equity valuations expand and S&P 500 returns turn positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rates are stable or rising, because the valuation channel is not easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior month, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy rates are effectively tighter and equity multiples compress. That combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which leads investors to price slower earnings growth and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when unemployment is flat or falling.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after unemployment rises by the stated threshold, the SPX has positive 20d returns in at least 60% of occurrences or the average 20d return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and broader real activity, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when industrial production is contracting or flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not above baseline when INDPRO is rising on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending, which should improve near-term revenue expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales growth does not translate into marketable demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of episodes, or if the conditional mean return is not materially above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST signals housing demand slowdown and weaker future consumption, which pressures cyclical earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when the housing decline is persistent.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are positive in most episodes after persistent HOUST weakness, or if the signal's 3-year rolling IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum deteriorates and risk assets reprice lower. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because the shock is interpreted as near-term growth loss.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS slowdown and UNRATE increase do not precede negative 5d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive policy regime, high FEDFUNDS combined with stagnant CPIAUCSL implies tight real rates and weaker discount support for equities, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when inflation is falling fast enough to offset policy tightness.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread versus unconditional returns is not at least -0.5 percentage points on average.
